Freedom2 – EN C performance in an EN B package

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.19.5″ _module_preset=”default” custom_margin=”||14px|||” global_colors_info=”{}”]

Yes. Freedom2 offers unprecedented performance gains for the EN B class.

A glider packed with state-of-the-art tech.

  • Hybrid 3-2 line layout
  • Efficient rear riser steering
  • Advanced shark nose airfoil
  • 4 ways split A attachment point VPA (Variable pressure A-Attachment)
  •  Semi light weight construction

Comfort in flight is paramount to fly a glider at its maximum and we took special attention to that.

Comfort in flight is only achieved with combination of many factors, such as:
Pitch stability, airfoil solidity, easy and efficient rear riser steering, easy launching and good flare, good top end speed, excellent glide, refined roll and bank in thermals, good feedback pressure through brake line and handles, light speed bar pressure and light and durable canopy.
We have addressed all those points in detail on the Freedom2.
An exciting and sporty package with EN B safety.

In addition to the hybrid 3-2 liner technology which reduces drag drastically offering huge gains in glide, Freedom2 also has a new shark-nose.

Unique for the Freedom2, we are introducing a new 4 ways split-A 3D A-attachment point system.
Adding a new level of solidity and collapse resistance to the EN B class. We call it VPA (Variable pressure A-Attachment).

The new 4 ways A-attachment point enhances the structural stability of the leading edge keeping the AoA positive, even when the glider’s AoA is at negative angles. It’s a better solution geometrically and it also helps in sharing the forces more evenly throughout the leading-edge span. The increased solidity is noticeable, especially when gliding at speed in turbulent air.

The 3D A-Attachment point creates what we call VPA (Variable pressure A-Attachment). At trim speed, the pressure is more even in all the 4 split attachment points.
When launching, during the pulling of the inflation phase, the pressure is at the very front lines of the “4-line split”. Resulting in quick and easy inflations.

It’s at speed where the VPA really shines as it shifts the pressure to the rear lines of the “4-line split”.

The change in geometry turns the leading edge more stable as the new attachment points offer better structural support at those angles, when inherently the leading edge is supposed to be “softer”.

It’s under that flying configuration (gliding with the speed bar applied) that we observe the most critical AoA and It’s right there where the VPA works at its best and that’s the reason the VPA was created. It aids the pressure shift to the rear lines of the “4-line split”. Mimicking the same solution given to higher-end gliders where the A attachment points are placed further back, which helps with the center-of-mass shift to the front lines, resulting in a stronger and more collapse resistance leading edge in lower AoA.

Freedom2 has a true racing pedigree with oustanding handling and agility. It’s also an incredibly fun wing to fly.

HYBRID 3 TO 2-LINE LAYOUT

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ use_custom_gutter=”on” gutter_width=”2″ make_equal=”on” _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” max_width=”1110px” custom_padding=”40px||0px|||” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_image src=”https://www.flowparagliders.com.au/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/hybrid-3-2-line-layout-1.jpg” title_text=”hybrid-3-2-line-layout” align=”center” _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” custom_margin=”||34px|||” global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” text_font=”||||||||” custom_margin=”||14px|||” global_colors_info=”{}”]The hybrid 3-2 line layout brings drastic drag reduction in comparison to the traditional 3-line layout.

Ultimately translating to increased gains in performance.[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” text_font=”||||||||” custom_margin=”||14px|||” global_colors_info=”{}”]

  • Better glide
  • More solidity
  • More efficiency
  • Higher top-end speed
  • Increased solidity at higher speeds

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row use_custom_gutter=”on” gutter_width=”2″ make_equal=”on” _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” max_width=”1110px” custom_padding=”50px||0px|||” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” header_font=”Oswald|600||on|||||” header_text_align=”center” header_text_color=”#FFFFFF” header_line_height=”1.3em” header_3_font=”Gruppo|700|||||||” header_3_text_align=”left” header_3_text_color=”#FFFFFF” header_3_font_size=”17px” header_4_font=”Oswald|700||on|||||” header_4_text_align=”center” header_4_text_color=”#171a25″ header_4_font_size=”40px” header_4_letter_spacing=”-0.2px” background_color=”#000000″ module_alignment=”center” custom_padding=”14px||5px|20px|false|false” header_4_font_size_tablet=”” header_4_font_size_phone=”30px” header_4_font_size_last_edited=”on|phone” global_colors_info=”{}”]

ADVANCED SHARK NOSE

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ use_custom_gutter=”on” gutter_width=”2″ make_equal=”on” _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” max_width=”1110px” custom_padding=”40px||0px|||” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_image src=”https://www.flowparagliders.com.au/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/advanced-shark-nose-1.jpg” title_text=”advanced-shark-nose” align=”center” _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” custom_margin=”||34px|||” global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” text_font=”||||||||” custom_margin=”||14px|||” global_colors_info=”{}”]Our advance shark nose profile is a pitch stable airfoil derived from our high end 2-liners which means:[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” text_font=”||||||||” custom_margin=”||14px|||” global_colors_info=”{}”]

  • More solidity
  • Higher internal pressure
  • Less oscillations
  • More efficient glides
  • Higher Comfort

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row use_custom_gutter=”on” gutter_width=”2″ make_equal=”on” _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” max_width=”1110px” custom_padding=”50px||0px|||” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” header_font=”Oswald|600||on|||||” header_text_align=”center” header_text_color=”#FFFFFF” header_line_height=”1.3em” header_3_font=”Gruppo|700||on|||||” header_3_text_align=”left” header_3_text_color=”#FFFFFF” header_3_font_size=”17px” header_4_font=”Oswald|700||on|||||” header_4_text_align=”center” header_4_text_color=”#171a25″ header_4_font_size=”40px” header_4_letter_spacing=”-0.2px” background_color=”#000000″ module_alignment=”center” custom_padding=”14px||5px|20px|false|false” header_4_font_size_tablet=”” header_4_font_size_phone=”30px” header_4_font_size_last_edited=”on|phone” global_colors_info=”{}”]

3D Cut Optimization and Advanced sail tensioning

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ use_custom_gutter=”on” gutter_width=”2″ make_equal=”on” _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” max_width=”1110px” custom_padding=”40px||0px|||” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_image src=”https://www.flowparagliders.com.au/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/3d-cut-optmisation.jpg” title_text=”3d-cut-optmisation” align=”center” _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” custom_margin=”||34px|||” global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.19.5″ _module_preset=”default” text_font=”||||||||” custom_margin=”||14px|||” global_colors_info=”{}”]Each panel and diagonals were structurally dimensioned and thoroughly computer analyzed to offer the most suitable chord and span-wise sail tension. The result is a canopy with less spanwise distortion giving a more taught and direct feel. Offering just enough flex characteristics to give the ideal feedback.

A well-tensioned sail is especially important to offer the pilot the right amount of feedback and feel of the air. It is specially important while thermaling to feel the nuances in areas of lift and sink more precisely, helping to find lift easier to stay in the core for longer.[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row use_custom_gutter=”on” gutter_width=”2″ make_equal=”on” _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” max_width=”1110px” custom_padding=”50px||0px|||” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” header_font=”Oswald|600||on|||||” header_text_align=”center” header_text_color=”#FFFFFF” header_line_height=”1.3em” header_3_font=”Gruppo|700||on|||||” header_3_text_align=”left” header_3_text_color=”#FFFFFF” header_3_font_size=”17px” header_4_font=”Oswald|700||on|||||” header_4_text_align=”center” header_4_text_color=”#171a25″ header_4_font_size=”40px” header_4_letter_spacing=”-0.2px” background_color=”#000000″ module_alignment=”center” custom_padding=”14px||5px|20px|false|false” header_4_font_size_tablet=”” header_4_font_size_phone=”30px” header_4_font_size_last_edited=”on|phone” global_colors_info=”{}”]

VPA system (VARIABLE PREASSURE A-ATACHMENT)

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ use_custom_gutter=”on” gutter_width=”2″ make_equal=”on” _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” max_width=”1110px” custom_padding=”40px||0px|||” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_image src=”https://www.flowparagliders.com.au/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/VPA-system-diagram-3.jpg” title_text=”VPA-system-diagram” align=”center” _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” custom_margin=”||34px|||” global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” text_font=”||||||||” custom_margin=”||14px|||” global_colors_info=”{}”]

The new 4 ways A-attachment VPA (variable position A-attachment point) enhances the structural stability of the leading edge keeping the AoA positive, even when the glider’s AoA is at negative angles.

It’s a better solution geometrically and it also helps in sharing the forces more evenly throughout the leading-edge span. The increased solidity is noticeable, especially when gliding at speed in turbulent air.

When speed bar is applied the VPA shifts the pressure to the rear lines of the “4-line split”.

The change in geometry turns the leading edge more stable as the new attachment points offer better structural support at those angles, we observe at most critical AoA (ex. gliding at full bar) it’s where the VPA works at its best.

It aids the pressure shift to the rear lines of the “4-line split”.

Mimicking the same solution given to higher-end gliders where the A attachment points are placed further back, which helps with the center-of-mass shift to the front lines, resulting in a stronger and more collapse resistance leading edge in lower AoA.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row use_custom_gutter=”on” gutter_width=”2″ make_equal=”on” _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” max_width=”1110px” custom_padding=”50px||0px|||” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” header_font=”Oswald|600||on|||||” header_text_align=”center” header_text_color=”#FFFFFF” header_line_height=”1.3em” header_3_font=”Gruppo|700||on|||||” header_3_text_align=”left” header_3_text_color=”#FFFFFF” header_3_font_size=”17px” header_4_font=”Oswald|700||on|||||” header_4_text_align=”center” header_4_text_color=”#171a25″ header_4_font_size=”40px” header_4_letter_spacing=”-0.2px” background_color=”#000000″ module_alignment=”center” custom_padding=”14px||5px|20px|false|false” header_4_font_size_tablet=”” header_4_font_size_phone=”30px” header_4_font_size_last_edited=”on|phone” global_colors_info=”{}”]

NEW RACING ARC

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ use_custom_gutter=”on” gutter_width=”2″ make_equal=”on” _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” max_width=”1110px” custom_padding=”40px||0px|||” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_image src=”https://www.flowparagliders.com.au/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/racing-ARC-diagram.jpg” title_text=”racing-ARC-diagram” align=”center” _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” custom_margin=”||34px|||” global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.19.5″ _module_preset=”default” text_font=”||||||||” custom_margin=”||14px|||” global_colors_info=”{}”]Freedom2 has a new redesigned arc offering excellent roll authority for thermaling and “centering” thermals.

Near the tips we have added a fair amount of wash-out to optimise glide angle and roll stability when gliding at speed.

The new arc also helps with overall line savings in comparison to version 1.

The new arc is inspired in our high-end gliders, it has a performance oriented design.[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ module_id=”colours” _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” custom_padding=”0px||80px||false|false” border_color_top=”#2B59C0″ border_color_bottom=”#2b59c0″ global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_row use_custom_gutter=”on” gutter_width=”2″ make_equal=”on” _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” max_width=”1110px” custom_padding=”50px||0px|||” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.17.4″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.16″ _module_preset=”default” header_font=”Oswald|600||on|||||” header_text_align=”center” header_text_color=”#FFFFFF” header_line_height=”1.3em” header_3_font=”Gruppo|700|||||||” header_3_text_align=”left” header_3_text_color=”#FFFFFF” header_3_font_size=”17px” header_4_font=”Oswald|700||on|||||” header_4_text_align=”center” header_4_text_color=”#171a25″ header_4_font_size=”40px” header_4_letter_spacing=”-0.2px” background_color=”#000000″ module_alignment=”center” custom_padding=”14px||5px|20px|false|false” header_4_font_size_tablet=”” header_4_font_size_phone=”30px” header_4_font_size_last_edited=”on|phone” global_colors_info=”{}”]

excerpt.rendered: TECHNICAL DATA TECHNOLOGY COLOURS DOWNLOADS Freedom2 – EN C performance in an EN B packageYes. Freedom2 offers unprecedented performance gains for the EN B class. A glider packed with state-of-the-art tech. Hybrid 3-2 line layout Efficient rear riser steering Advanced shark nose airfoil 4 ways split A attachment point VPA (Variable pressure A-Attachment)  Semi light […]
